name: Cornish Witchcraft
description: Cornish Witchcraft, deeply rooted in the Celtic traditions of Cornwall, is a magical practice interwoven with the land, sea, and the spirits of ancestors. Unlike some modern Wiccan traditions, it emphasizes a close relationship with the natural world, particularly the coastline and moors. Its origins are a blend of pre-Christian beliefs, folk magic, and cunning craft practices passed down through generations, often within families. Cornish witches, known as 'pellars' or 'wise women/men', traditionally worked with charms, herbs, and divination to heal the sick, protect livestock, influence weather (especially sea weather), and provide blessings. They also dealt with curses and hexes, though this aspect is often shrouded in secrecy. The practice involves working with the spirits of the land, including the Bucca (sea spirits) and the piskies (fairies), requiring respect and offerings. Core elements include sea magic, working with the tides and coastal resources; herbalism, utilizing the medicinal properties of local plants; divination through scrying and other methods; and the use of charms and spells for various purposes. Cornish Witchcraft is less about structured rituals and more about practical magic tailored to specific needs, emphasizing personal connection and intuition within the Cornish landscape. The cultural significance lies in its connection to Cornish identity and the preservation of ancient traditions in a rapidly changing world. It often embraces the unique Cornish language and storytelling traditions to connect with the past.
subtitle: Magic of the Land, Sea, and Ancestors

keyMyths.title: The Bucca and the Fisherman
keyMyths.description: A fisherman makes a pact with the Bucca for a bountiful catch, but fails to uphold his end of the bargain, leading to misfortune. This story emphasizes the importance of honoring agreements with the spirits of the sea.

sources.author: Gemma Gary
sources.work: Traditional Witchcraft: A Cornish Book of Ways
sources.description: A modern exploration of Cornish witchcraft traditions.
sources.text: A modern exploration of Cornish witchcraft traditions.

sources.author: Kelvin I. Jones
sources.description: While focused on Arthurian myth, it provides insight into Celtic beliefs relevant to Cornish witchcraft.
